pkgmetadata.longdescription:
Donut Browser is an open source anti-detect browser. It supports unlimited isolated browser profiles with separate fingerprints, cookies, extensions and data, using Chromium (Wayfern) and Firefox (Camoufox) engines with advanced fingerprint spoofing. Includes per-profile proxy and WireGuard VPN support, a local REST API and Model Context Protocol server, profile groups, import from Chrome/Firefox/Edge/Brave, cookie and extension management, optional end-to-end encrypted cloud sync, and zero telemetry.
Note: this is a prebuilt -bin package built on webkit2gtk-4.1. On Wayland the DMA-BUF renderer in webkit2gtk may crash with "Gdk-Message: Error 71 (Protocol error) dispatching to Wayland display". The bundled .desktop entry launches the browser with WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 to avoid this. When starting from a terminal under Wayland, set WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 manually. GDK_BACKEND=x11 (XWayland) also works, but may render the application window as a black screen on some setups - prefer the WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 workaround.
